# Commands: QInstruments BioShake

Generated by `/v1b1-protocol QInstruments BioShake bytestream tests/smoke/inputs/bioshake-session-1.pcap "<intent>"` on 2026-04-25. 6 entries, keyed to v1b1 transport form `command-class`. Framing (locked at Q&A turn 1, candidate A): `0xAA <len:uint8> <opcode:uint8> <payload> <crc16:uint16-LE> 0xFE`. CRC-16/XMODEM (poly `0x1021`, init `0x0000`, no reflection, no XOR-out) over bytes `[opcode .. payload-end]`, serialized little-endian. `len` counts opcode + payload + CRC bytes.

## LockPlate

**Verb / Opcode**: 0xAA <len=0x03> 0x30 <crc16_lo> <crc16_hi> 0xFE

**Transport form**: command-class

**Confidence**: confirmed

**Request shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x03` (opcode + 2 CRC bytes; no payload) |
| opcode | uint8 | `0x30` for LockPlate |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Response shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x04` (opcode + status + 2 CRC bytes) |
| opcode | uint8 | echoed `0x30` |
| status | uint8 | `0x00` = ack |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode, status]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Side effects**: Engages the plate clamp. Fire-and-forget command followed by an ack response. No motion is implied; safe to send while shaking.

**Evidence**:

- Capture timestamp range: 12:34:56.300000 – 12:34:59.305000
- Request packet indices: [6, 18, 30] (`aa 03 30 53 36 fe`)
- Response packet indices: [7, 19, 31] (`aa 04 30 00 95 05 fe`)
- Disambiguated by: turn 1 (framing A vs B → A), turn 4 (lock/unlock parameterless shape confirmed; opcodes 0x30 / 0x31).

## QueryStatus

**Verb / Opcode**: 0xAA <len=0x03> 0x40 <crc16_lo> <crc16_hi> 0xFE

**Transport form**: command-class

**Confidence**: confirmed

**Request shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x03` (opcode + 2 CRC bytes; no payload) |
| opcode | uint8 | `0x40` for QueryStatus |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Response shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x05` (opcode + status_byte + temp_byte + 2 CRC bytes) |
| opcode | uint8 | echoed `0x40` |
| status_byte.motion_state | uint4 (high nibble) | `0` = idle, `1` = shaking |
| status_byte.lock_state | uint4 (low nibble) | `0` = unlocked, `1` = locked |
| temperature | uint8 (BCD) | each decimal digit in a nibble; observed `0x37` = 37 °C |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode, status_byte, temperature]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Side effects**: Polls the device for current motion + lock + temperature state. Read-only; safe to call at any time. Returns synchronously within ~5 ms.

**Evidence**:

- Capture timestamp range: 12:34:56.500000 – 12:34:59.505000
- Request packet indices: [10, 22, 34] (`aa 03 40 c4 48 fe`)
- Response packet indices: [11, 23, 35] (`aa 05 40 11 37 5b 6b fe`; `0x11` = motion=1 + lock=1; `0x37` = 37 °C BCD)
- Disambiguated by: turn 1 (framing), turn 5 (status byte bitfield decomposition + temp byte BCD encoding).

## SetTemperature

**Verb / Opcode**: 0xAA <len=0x04> 0x20 <temp_bcd> <crc16_lo> <crc16_hi> 0xFE

**Transport form**: command-class

**Confidence**: confirmed

**Request shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x04` (opcode + temperature + 2 CRC bytes) |
| opcode | uint8 | `0x20` for SetTemperature |
| temperature | uint8 (BCD) | target °C; each decimal digit in a nibble; observed `0x37` = 37 °C |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode, temperature]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Response shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x04` (opcode + status + 2 CRC bytes) |
| opcode | uint8 | echoed `0x20` |
| status | uint8 | `0x00` = ack |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode, status]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Side effects**: Sets the temperature setpoint and begins active heating/cooling toward it. Does not block. Acknowledgement is immediate; reaching setpoint takes seconds–minutes depending on delta. A subsequent `QueryStatus` reports the live temperature.

**Evidence**:

- Capture timestamp range: 12:34:56.200000 – 12:34:59.205000
- Request packet indices: [4, 16, 28] (`aa 04 20 37 52 40 fe`)
- Response packet indices: [5, 17, 29] (`aa 04 20 00 e6 06 fe`)
- Disambiguated by: turn 1 (framing), turn 3 (BCD vs binary uint8 → BCD; matches the contributor's intent of `set_temperature 37` = `0x37`).

## StartShake

**Verb / Opcode**: 0xAA <len=0x05> 0x10 <speed_lo> <speed_hi> <crc16_lo> <crc16_hi> 0xFE

**Transport form**: command-class

**Confidence**: confirmed

**Request shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x05` (opcode + 2 speed bytes + 2 CRC bytes) |
| opcode | uint8 | `0x10` for StartShake |
| speed | uint16 (little-endian) | target RPM; observed `0x01F4` = 500 RPM |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode, speed_lo, speed_hi]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Response shape**:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| start_byte | uint8 | always `0xAA` |
| length | uint8 | always `0x04` (opcode + status + 2 CRC bytes) |
| opcode | uint8 | echoed `0x10` |
| status | uint8 | `0x00` = ack |
| checksum | uint16 (little-endian) | CRC-16/XMODEM over `[opcode, status]` |
| end_byte | uint8 | always `0xFE` |

**Side effects**: Begins shaking at the given speed. Does not block. A subsequent `StopShake` is required to halt; sending `StartShake` again replaces the speed without intermediate stop.

**Evidence**:

- Capture timestamp range: 12:34:56.000000 – 12:34:59.005000
- Request packet indices: [0, 12, 24] (`aa 05 10 f4 01 47 8c fe`)
- Response packet indices: [1, 13, 25] (`aa 04 10 00 73 03 fe`)
- Disambiguated by: turn 1 (framing A vs B → A; CRC-16 confirmed against vendor doc fragment), turn 2 (speed endianness → little; `0xF4 0x01` = 500 LE matches the contributor's intent of `start_shake at 500 RPM`).
